targets.failure_pattern: The deck begins like an engineering report, with proof density and mechanism labels visible before the audience has an orientation moment.
targets.desired_behavior: The opening should pause on a sparse native visual field, then reveal the editable headline before any proof marker appears.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT text and shape objects for the opening field and headline.
Attach motion metadata describing attention reset, hold, and delayed proof reveal.
Record selected beat ids, motion target ids, visual component ids, density counts, and trace notes.
targets.qa_probe: A reviewer can see the attention reset from the contact sheet and verify the reveal order in trace metadata.

targets.failure_pattern: The slide says the design improved but never shows the change as a visible original before and after object.
targets.desired_behavior: The before thumbnail should reveal first, the after thumbnail should reveal second with a larger proof object, and the delta label should explain the edit.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT thumbnail frames, labels, arrows, and proof objects.
Attach motion metadata for before reveal, after reveal, and delta lockup.
Record selected beat ids, visual target ids, visual component ids, deleted content, and trace notes.
targets.qa_probe: The transformation direction is visible before reading the body copy.

targets.failure_pattern: Proof appears as pasted explanation or equal panels, so the audience cannot tell which object is being built.
targets.desired_behavior: The proof should build from headline to object to route marker, with a small pause between claim and evidence.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT headline, proof object, route marker, and rhythm strip.
Attach motion metadata for build order, pause, and detail routing.
Record selected transcript anchors, beat ids, motion target ids, and trace notes.
targets.qa_probe: The proof object enters as the main build, while transcript-like detail stays out of the slide body.

targets.failure_pattern: The climax repeats the same scale and density as the proof slide, so no public-demo moment is created.
targets.desired_behavior: The climax should scale one native outcome object above prior proof objects and let minor labels become subordinate.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT outcome object, comparison labels, and supporting shapes.
Attach motion metadata for scale emphasis and label reduction.
Record selected beat ids, visual component ids, object scale ratio, and trace notes.
targets.qa_probe: The climax object is the largest object in the sequence and can be identified from the contact sheet.

targets.failure_pattern: The close adds a final status grid instead of converting the outcome into a decision handoff.
targets.desired_behavior: The close should create a handoff from outcome state to public-blocked release gate and one next decision line.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT release gate, handoff line, and optional mini-preview object.
Attach motion metadata for handoff reveal and final pause.
Record selected beat ids, release gate inputs, sequence component ids, and trace notes.
targets.qa_probe: The close reads as a decision handoff and keeps public release blocked.

qa_gates: each motion target references known beat ids
each motion target references known visual targets and native components
each motion target requires native editable objects, motion metadata, and trace fields
motion targets contain no copied source media references
public release remains blocked until render, provenance, and human approval
